assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met |'. Detailed findings in report.md lines 110-141 show each criterion individually as PASS: Criterion 1 (single license filter), Criterion 2 (comma-separated filter), Criterion 3 (400 validation), Criterion 4 (pagination integration), Criterion 5 (unchanged response shape). Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each contain individual PASS verdicts with code-level evidence.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 PR files match the task specification exactly (2 modified, 1 created) |'. Detailed file-by-file comparison table in report.md lines 33-39 shows: list.rs (Files to Modify -> Modified, Match: Yes), service/mod.rs (Files to Modify -> Modified, Match: Yes), tests/api/package.rs (Files to Create -> New file, Match: Yes). Out-of-scope files: none. Unimplemented files: none.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR |'. report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md and report.md. All content is verification analysis — code snippets appear only as quoted evidence within the analysis, not as modifications. The report reads the diff and verifies against criteria without making any code changes.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and 'All checks pass. The PR correctly implements the license filter...' but takes no merge action. The report presents a summary table and detailed findings for human review. No merge commands, API calls, or merge actions appear anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines |'. Detailed findings in report.md lines 71-93 list six scanned categories (hardcoded passwords, API keys/tokens, private keys/certificates, environment/config files, cloud provider credentials, database credentials) all with 'no matches'. Evidence describes added lines as Rust imports, struct fields, SPDX validation logic, SeaORM queries, and test code.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | 1 new test file with 4 test functions; no modified or deleted test files |'. Detailed findings in report.md lines 193-198 confirm: 'tests/api/package.rs is listed under "Files to Create" in the task and appears as new file mode 100644 in the diff', 'No other test files were modified', 'Classification: ADDITIVE (purely new test coverage, no existing coverage altered)'.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md detailed findings are organized under four domain headers: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 25-65) containing Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (WARN); 'Security' (lines 69-93) containing Sensitive Pattern Scan (PASS); 'Correctness' (lines 97-147) containing CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A); 'Style/Conventions' (lines 151-198) containing Repetitive Test Detection (PASS), Test Documentation (PASS), Eval Quality (N/A), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). Summary table row for Test Quality (line 13) combines sub-verdicts: 'All tests documented, no repetitive patterns detected. Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: file-by-file comparison table (report.md lines 33-37) listing each file with Task Spec, PR Status, and Match columns. Security: line-level scan listing six categories with 'no matches' and itemizing what the added lines actually contain (report.md lines 76-92). Correctness: per-criterion verification (report.md lines 110-141) with code-level evidence including function names, code snippets, and test assertions for each of the 5 acceptance criteria. Style/Conventions: test quality table (report.md lines 165-172) comparing setup/action/assertions across 4 test functions, plus per-test doc comment quotes (lines 179-184).

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 187-189: '#### Eval Quality -- N/A' with 'No eval result reviews exist on this PR. No eval pass rate or assertion data to analyze.' Summary table line 13: '| Test Quality | PASS | All tests documented, no repetitive patterns detected. Eval Quality: N/A |' — Test Quality is PASS based on Repetitive Test Detection and Test Documentation alone, with Eval Quality explicitly noted as N/A and not affecting the combined verdict.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 187-189). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks in any output file. No sub-tasks of any kind are created — report.md line 6 states 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR |' and '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |'. These appear in the summary table (lines 4-16) as top-level rows before the domain-specific detailed findings sections. They are not nested under any of the four domain headings (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions), confirming they are determined independently by the orchestrator.
